next_angles: wire PLR/periodic numerical branch into FT-Transformer feature tokenizer (cross-link to tabular-transformers)
control target-aware (PLEt) bin leakage with out-of-fold bin construction
stress-test per-feature numerical embeddings at 70+ numerical features for param-blowup / sequence-length cost
compare PLR vs AutoDis vs raw-scalar across heterogeneous feature distributions
numerical-embeddings.novelty_remaining: medium — canonical numerical recipe (PLR/PLE) established; open questions in scaling, leakage control, and per-feature functional-form heterogeneity
numerical-embeddings.actionability_for_the_topic: high — defines the continuous-feature half of a 70+-mixed-feature tokenizer

next_angles: Compare TabTransformer (categorical-only contextualizer, raw-concat numerics) vs FT-Transformer (numerical tokenized too) head-to-head on numerical-heavy tables to quantify the numerical-bypass cost
Reuse the per-column-identifier-by-concatenation trick + per-column MISSING embedding row as the categorical leg of a 70+-mixed tokenizer
Test concatenated per-column identifier and generator-free per-column RTD pretraining at 70+ columns (and on binned numerical features)
swap numerical branch for PLR/periodic embeddings (link to numerical-embeddings route)
efficient/linear attention to break the O(k^2) feature-count wall
high-cardinality compression for the categorical branch (the Grinsztajn open question)
large-data regime where the tree-vs-DL gap narrows (TabPFN v2 / foundation models)
does the rotation-invariance failure persist once high-cardinality + temporal channels are present
use LeoGrin/tabular-benchmark harness as a fixed-budget yardstick for our own tokenizer
Test T2G FR-Graph interaction prior at 70-300 features where dense attention degrades
Compose T2G Graph Estimator with PLR/periodic numerical embeddings to verify tokenizer/interaction decoupling
re-add numerical embeddings into TabR/ModernNCA to isolate retrieval
feature-selection front-end for wide tables
adopt time-based splits as standard validation harness

Test SPA + Feat-Mix on a real 70+-feature high-cardinality table (TabReD) atop an FT-Transformer with PLR numerical embeddings and learned categorical tokens, to separate the transferable regularizers from ExcelFormer's lossy CatBoost-scalarization tokenizer
Compare static-MI SPA mask vs a learned/periodically-refreshed importance mask at f>=70
Re-add PLR numerical embeddings into TabR/ModernNCA and ablate retrieval vs tokenizer credit on wide drifting tables

Pair Trompt's O(P x C) prompt-importance gate with PLR/periodic numerical embeddings and stress-test at 70+ high-cardinality features (its naive scalar->Dense numerics + Grinsztajn-filtered eval leave the wide/high-card regime unverified)
Quantify Trompt's B x P x C x d activation-memory constant on wide tables — the O(P x C) framing hides a large constant at P=128
tabular-transformers.novelty_remaining: medium — route now has the foundational categorical-contextualizer anchor (TabTransformer) plus FT-Transformer, SAINT, T2G, and the benchmark 'why'. The lineage (entity-embeddings -> TabTransformer column tokens -> FT-Transformer numerical tokenization) is mapped; open: high-cardinality compression, numerical-heavy contextualization, temporal/large-data.
tabular-transformers.evidence_quality: high (NeurIPS 2022 D&B, 45 datasets, ~20k compute-hrs random search, raw results released)
tabular-transformers.actionability_for_the_topic: high as motivation/rules-of-the-game (per-feature tokens, high-frequency numerical embeddings, feature selection at scale); explicitly does NOT cover high-cardinality / missing / temporal features

next_angles: stress-test per-field embeddings at 70+ features vs attention tokenizers
out-of-fold leakage-controlled embedding transfer vs CatBoost/target encoding
discretize-then-embed continuous features (paper's future work)
add OOV/cold-start bucket
empirically test MinHash-into-transformer projection vs entity-embedding+OOV
compare lexical (n-gram) vs semantic (text-embedding) similarity for dirty categoricals
many-feature cost ablation: 20+ MinHash-encoded string columns in a transformer tokenizer
categorical-high-cardinality.novelty_remaining: medium — route now has the foundational entity-embedding anchor PLUS the first vocabulary-free/stateless string encoder (MinHash/Gamma-Poisson); open: MinHash-into-transformer projection, lexical-vs-semantic similarity, and many-feature cost
categorical-high-cardinality.evidence_quality: high (peer-reviewed TKDE, 17+ real datasets, productionized in skrub/dirty_cat); the 2016 entity-embedding anchor remains single-dataset
categorical-high-cardinality.actionability_for_the_topic: high — vocabulary-free OOV-robust fixed-width MinHash encoder is the missing high-cardinality primitive complementing entity embeddings (closed-vocab) and target encoding (label-leaking)

next_angles: Lift TabICL's shared Set-Transformer column embedder (ISAB hypernetwork emitting per-cell W,B, zero per-feature params) into a supervised wide-table tokenizer — the most reusable 70+-feature primitive found so far

Test TAE (target-aware embedding, TabICLv2) vs target/CatBoost encoding inside a supervised FT-Transformer over 70+ features — TAE only works where labeled context exists at inference (ICL), so adapting it to non-ICL supervised is open work
Probe the n*m^2 feature-count ceiling 100->1000 features (TabICLv2 still quadratic in m); both TabICL variants are pretrained <=100 features so 70+ sits at/above the training ceiling
Deep-read RealTabPFN-2.5 / TabPFN-2.5 as the dethroned baselines TabICLv2 surpasses; verify the 'without tuning' headline net of 8-shuffle ensembling cost
Compare CARTE (real-knowledge LM prior) vs TabPFN-v2/TabICL (synthetic-SCM prior) vs XTab as transfer paradigms; none yet proven at 70+ features with high-cardinality categorical INPUTS
Investigate whether RoPE-over-feature-axis (TabICL) or repeated-feature-grouping (TabICLv2) is the cleaner fix for representation collapse among identically-distributed features
tabular-foundation-models.novelty_remaining: medium — route now has four paradigms: TabPFN-v2 (per-cell two-way attention, synthetic SCM), CARTE (LM-on-colnames graphlet, real-knowledge prior), TabICL v1 (shared Set-Transformer column embedder + column-collapse-to-row-token + RoPE, O(m^2 n + n^2)), TabICL v2 (target-aware embedding + column-then-row attention + repeated-feature-grouping + QASSMax, current open SOTA). Open: high-cardinality categorical INPUTS, temporal fusion, and behavior strictly above the <=100-feature pretraining regime
tabular-foundation-models.evidence_quality: TabPFN-v2: medium-high (Nature, secondhand numbers). CARTE: high small-scale, unproven at 70+/large-n. TabICL v1: high (ICML 2025, TALENT 200 datasets, surpasses TabPFNv2+CatBoost on >10K-sample sets; full ablations, code+weights). TabICL v2: medium (arXiv 2026, surpasses RealTabPFN-2.5 on TabArena/TALENT, 10x faster; but pretraining code unreleased and gains depend on 8-estimator ensembling)
tabular-foundation-models.actionability_for_the_topic: high as transferable-ideas source — the shared distribution-aware column embedder + column-collapse-to-row-token (TabICL) is the cleanest 'ingest N heterogeneous features with zero per-feature params and pay sample-axis attention once' recipe; TAE is a cheap label-conditioning trick. BUT none solves high-cardinality categorical INPUTS or temporal fusion, and all sit at the edge of their <=100-feature training regime

next_angles: LIFT / GReaT as the other serialization paradigms (GReaT = generative row synthesis; LIFT = no-template fine-tune) to contrast against TabLLM's manual Text Template
Reconcile TabLLM (column names matter in very-few-shot) vs LIFT/Dinh-2022 (could NOT confirm column-name importance at fractional data) — sample-efficiency is task/LLM-dependent
Bolt a numeric-specific embedding (Gorishniy-2022 PLR) onto serialized numerics instead of raw digit strings (authors' own future work)
Quantify the token-budget wall: at what feature count / verbosity does serialization force lossy truncation, and how does that compare to per-feature-token sequence length
Use the permuted-names / only-values ablation protocol as a general test that ANY tokenizer actually uses feature names + fine-grained values
Apply GReaT random feature-order-permutation training to a per-feature-token (non-LLM) tabular model to get an order-free arbitrary-conditioning encoder
Bolt PLR numeric embeddings onto GReaT serialized numerics instead of raw digit strings (shared with TabLLM future work)
stress-test RMT+IFA at 70-200 features (TP-BERTa pretrained <=32 features; IFA makes seq-len=feature-count but the regime is untested)
compare RMT vs PLR/periodic numerical embeddings on a non-LM transformer
test on high-cardinality non-semantic id columns the paper excluded
swap C4.5 target-aware bins for unsupervised/out-of-fold bins to remove RMT leakage risk before relying on it
llm-tabular-serialization.novelty_remaining: medium-high — route now has BOTH the discriminative anchor (TabLLM serialize->classify) and the generative anchor (GReaT serialize->generate). Open: LIFT paradigm, numeric-encoding upgrade, and whether the permutation/order-invariance trick transfers to non-LLM token-sequence tabular models.
llm-tabular-serialization.evidence_quality: high for TabLLM/GReaT mechanism; TP-BERTa adds a broad 145-dataset eval + clean ablations but a rank-only headline with default-HP-vs-tuned-baselines asymmetry. Topic-relevant scaling evidence stays NEGATIVE: GReaT caps 47 features, TP-BERTa pretrains <=32.
llm-tabular-serialization.actionability_for_the_topic: low as a literal 70+-feature LLM-serialization recipe; HIGH as a source of backbone-agnostic nuggets — RMT (magnitude-aware numerical tokens) and IFA (name+value fusion to one token) are both directly implementable, plus GReaT's order-permutation trick. Re-validate RMT+IFA at 70+ features and replace C4.5 bins with out-of-fold/unsupervised bins before relying on them.
llm-tabular-serialization.note: TP-BERTa is the LM-serialization route's strongest numerical-handling entry: target-aware magnitude tokens (RMT) + intra-feature attention (IFA) that collapses each feature's name+value to one token. Architecturally linear in feature count but only validated at <=32 pretrain features / low-dim downstream; categorical-dominated win, loses on all-numerical tables.

next_angles: Pair iTransformer joint-variate attention with PatchTST patch tokens (or PLR/periodic per-value embeddings) for finer temporal structure
Add a categorical/static token pathway to the inverted (variate-as-token) scheme for genuine mixed-type fusion
Test variate subsampling per batch at 70+ mixed features to keep O(N^2) attention affordable
graft VSN static-context gating onto FT-Transformer/PatchTST for missing static-fusion + feature-selection
stress-test VSN / variate-as-token at genuine 70+ heterogeneous feature width
add a categorical/static token pathway to TimeXer's role-asymmetric scheme for genuine mixed-type fusion (it is numeric/temporal-only today)
stress-test the single-global-bridge-token bottleneck vs multiple global tokens at 70+ interacting features (loses to iTransformer joint attention on high-interaction Traffic)
use role-asymmetric tokenization (fine tokens for target features, one coarse variate token per context feature + 1xC cross-attention) as a linear-in-context-feature-count lever at 70+ features
temporal-feature-tokenization.novelty_remaining: medium — three anchors now recorded: patching (PatchTST channel-independent), variable-selection/static-fusion (TFT), and variate-as-token joint attention (iTransformer). Open: mixed-type (categorical/static) fusion and per-value temporal numeric encoding at 70+ width
temporal-feature-tokenization.evidence_quality: high for mechanism (iTransformer SOTA on Traffic 862 / ECL 321 / PEMS 883 — concrete many-variate evidence); low for genuine mixed-type 70+-feature scaling (variates are all numeric)
temporal-feature-tokenization.actionability_for_the_topic: high — variate-as-token is the cleanest unification blueprint (each feature -> one token by a type-appropriate encoder -> one joint attention); plus two scaling/heterogeneity tools (per-token LayerNorm/RevIN, variate subsampling). Pitfall: 'variates' are numeric, not literal mixed-type heterogeneity | TimeXer adds two reusable levers: (1) role-asymmetric tokenization — don't tokenize every feature at the same granularity; give the feature(s) you care about fine patch/per-value tokens and collapse many context features to ONE coarse variate token each, connected by cheap 1xC cross-attention (LINEAR in context-feature count); (2) a learnable global bridge token as an aggregation hub. Pitfall: the single global token bottlenecks on high-interaction many-variate sets, and there is no categorical/static pathway yet.

next_angles: Benchmark AutoDis soft-codebook (H=20-40 meta-embeddings + temperature softmax + weighted-avg) vs PLE/PLR under FT-Transformer on a 70+-feature TabReD/TabArena task (AUC + params/latency)
Anneal AutoDis temperature tau->0 to recover discrete codes and compare against RQ-VAE semantic IDs (tiger-rqvae) — soft-codebook vs hard residual quantization on the same tabular embedding
Decide what embedding to quantize for RQ-VAE (per-row? per-field-group? per-temporal-patch?) — TIGER gives no guidance
Use RQ-VAE shared codebooks to bound high-cardinality categorical vocabulary with graceful OOV (vs entity-embedding tables / hashing)
Test AutoDis adaptive-temperature network on highly heterogeneous many-feature sets (tau optimum varies 1e-5..4e-3 across datasets)
Codebook-collapse mitigation (k-means init, balanced assignment) at high cardinality
discretization-vq.novelty_remaining: high — route now has two anchors: RQ-VAE semantic IDs (hard residual quantization, recsys-native) and AutoDis (soft/learned per-field discretization, CTR-native). The tabular-prediction application of either at 70+ features, and the soft-vs-hard codebook comparison, remain open.
discretization-vq.evidence_quality: medium — AutoDis: strong mechanism + ablations (KDD 2021) but CTR-only, small absolute AUC gains, beaten by PLE/PLR on general tabular (Gorishniy 2022). RQ-VAE: strong mechanism but recsys-retrieval-only, no tabular-prediction evidence.
discretization-vq.actionability_for_the_topic: medium — AutoDis gives a cheap, parameter-bounded soft numerical tokenizer (bridges to VQ/semantic-ID); RQ-VAE gives a bounded shared codebook for high-cardinality compression. Both need re-benchmarking on 70+-mixed tabular before adoption; neither is the default over PLE/PLR yet.

next_angles: Test MIMO/TabM head over an FT-Transformer or wide tabular MLP on 70+ mixed features (TabReD); sweep M in {2,3,4} and rho in {0,0.3,0.6} vs a deep ensemble
Run conditional-variance subnetwork-separation diagnostic on tokenized tabular embeddings

TabM does NOT do explicit feature interaction/selection — it scales via flat-concat linear-cost MLP ensembling; use it as the scalable backbone BASELINE, not as an interaction method (DCN-V2/AutoInt/T2G/TabNet cover true interaction/selection)
Test MiniEnsemble (first-multiplicative-adapter-only, the load-bearing R) wrapped around an attention tokenizer to see if the cheap ensembling gain is MLP-specific or transferable to any backbone
Stack a high-cardinality + temporal tokenizer on a TabM backbone and ablate ensembling-gain vs tokenizer-gain (disentangle PLE credit from ensembling credit)
head-to-head DCN-V2 cross vs AutoInt attention on same 70-feature tokenizer at matched params (accuracy + wall-clock)
combine PLR/FT-Transformer numeric embeddings with low-rank cross layers on a dense 70-feature target
field-count scaling curve (10->120 fields) for full vs low-rank vs DCN-Mix to calibrate the low-rank-mandatory threshold
combine TabNet sparsemax selection front-end with PLR/FT-Transformer tokenizer; test faithfulness of M_agg importances at 70+ features
compare T2G FR-Graph learned-sparse interaction vs AutoInt/DCN-V2 explicit interaction at 70+ fields
wrap a real heterogeneous-feature tokenizer (PLR + category tokens) in BatchEnsemble adapters and measure accuracy/NLL/ECE vs single backbone and naive ensemble on a 70+-feature table
ablate adapter placement: tokenizer embedding layers vs backbone vs both
test cheap-ensemble calibration under tabular distribution shift (TabReD-style temporal split)
compose AMFormer's arithmetic block on top of an expressive numerical tokenizer (PLR/periodic) to test whether multiplicative interaction still helps beyond a raw-affine embedding
stress-test multiplicative-stream stability and a signed-log alternative at 70+ correlated features
re-evaluate the 'arithmetic interaction is necessary' claim on in-the-wild TabReD/TabArena tables rather than synthetic monomials
feature-interaction-selection.novelty_remaining: medium — now has attention (AutoInt), cross-operator (DCN-V2), instance-wise selection (TabNet), learned interaction-graph (T2G), AND a linear-cost MLP-ensemble scalable backbone (TabM, current benchmark #1). Open: matched-compute scaling at 70+ fields; whether explicit interaction/selection ever beats a strong flat-concat MLP+ensemble on wide heterogeneous tables.
feature-interaction-selection.evidence_quality: high mechanism/ablation evidence across AutoInt (CIKM'19), DCN-V2 (WWW'21), TabNet (AAAI'21, strong synthetic+mechanism but supervised wins likely under-tuned), T2G (12 datasets, 15-seed, tuned, multiple ablations, <=93 features). None at genuine 70+ general-tabular features with high-cardinality/temporal inputs.
feature-interaction-selection.actionability_for_the_topic: high — TabM is the recommended cheap, linear-cost backbone (and near-free accuracy wrapper via BatchEnsemble adapters) to put a proper heterogeneous-feature tokenizer ON TOP of; PLE per-feature numerical embeddings give the largest single boost. But TabM is NOT a tokenizer (one-hot categoricals are high-cardinality-hostile) and NOT a feature-interaction method. | AMFormer adds a backbone-agnostic drop-in arithmetic block (explicit multiplicative attention via log-space+exp, parallel to additive attention) and a prompt-token query trick giving linear-in-N attention; it contributes NO tokenizer of its own, so it composes with this topic's tokenizer choices. Re-validate beyond synthetic monomials and check multiplicative-stream stability before trusting the necessity claim.
